prompt_additions:
CRITICAL: The vertical ruling lines for £, s, and d are faint to invisible across the top and middle sections of the page. Use the clear alignment of the 'Total' row (Row 36) (36.7.9) as your primary reference point to establish and project the precise column boundaries for £, s, and d for all rows above. Pay close attention to the pence column, as its left boundary is often the most indistinct.

Row 10 ('Chace Hill') has a dark smudge over 'Hill'. Extract the text as best as possible, noting potential readability issues.

Rows 31 ('Pintinhill } Raper') and 32 ('Hopkins') represent a single logical entry spanning two visual lines, indicated by a vertical bracket on the left. The 'Raper' value applies to both 'Pintinhill' and 'Hopkins'. When extracting, combine these into one logical description field, e.g., 'Pintinhill & Hopkins (Raper)'.
